news.headline: US-Iran 60-Day Peace Roadmap → WTI Crude -2.23% to $74.07
news.what_happened: The US Treasury authorised the production, delivery and sale of Iranian oil and petroleum products for 60 days. The US and Iran agreed on a roadmap toward a final nuclear deal, boosting expectations of gradual Hormuz Strait supply recovery. WTI crude fell to $74.07, its lowest since early March.
news.why_it_matters: Crude -2.23% directly slashes fuel & raw-material costs for India's oil-dependent sectors. IndiGo saves ~₹350-400 Cr/year per $1 crude drop. Tyre makers (synthetic rubber, carbon black = 60-70% of RM) and paint companies (crude derivatives = 40-50% of RM) get immediate margin expansion. India's CAD also narrows, supporting the rupee.

news.headline: US Nasdaq +3.07%, S&P 500 +1.65% — Dow Hits Record 51,671
news.what_happened: US equity markets surged on June 22 as the Iran deal eased inflation fears. Nasdaq posted its biggest single-session gain in months (+3.07%), the S&P 500 rose 1.65%, and the Dow Jones hit a new record close of 51,671. Oil price relief is seen as a structural inflation-fighter.
news.why_it_matters: Indian IT stocks closely shadow Nasdaq moves. A 3%+ Nasdaq day historically translates to 1.5-2.5% IT sector move in India next session. USD/INR at 94.25 (rupee structurally weak vs history) means USD-revenue earners report outsized INR revenue. FII flows likely to continue into Indian large-caps.

news.headline: FII Net Buying ₹4,859 Cr on June 22 — 4th Consecutive Day of Inflows
news.what_happened: Foreign institutional investors were net buyers of ₹4,859 Cr in the cash segment on June 22. DIIs were net sellers of ₹1,160 Cr providing liquidity. This is the fourth consecutive session of meaningful FII inflows.
news.why_it_matters: Sustained FII buying lifts index heavyweights with high FII ownership (HDFCBANK, RELIANCE, ICICIBANK, INFY, TCS). Broad market breadth improves. Signals global risk-on rotation into India as an EM beneficiary of lower crude and stable macro.

news.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate at 5.25%; Says May Inflation Pickup is Transient
news.what_happened: The RBI kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% for the third consecutive meeting, maintaining a neutral stance. RBI's June bulletin stated inflation remains anchored below target despite the recent energy shock. GDP forecast for FY27 lowered to 6.6% from 6.9%.
news.why_it_matters: No near-term rate-hike risk from RBI removes a key overhang on rate-sensitive sectors. Bond yields may ease slightly, benefiting banks' treasury portfolios. NBFCs and housing finance see continued cheap-liquidity environment. However, RBI neutral stance (not dovish) limits any fresh upside catalyst for banks.

news.headline: Nikkei +1.58% to 72,379; Shanghai +1.8% — Asia Risk-On Broad
news.what_happened: Asian markets rallied strongly on June 23 morning: Nikkei 225 gained 1.58% to close at 72,379 — among its highest levels. Shanghai Composite rose 1.8%. Taiwan Taiex surged 2.7% to a record intraday high. Hang Seng lagged (-0.3%) after a technical death cross.
news.why_it_matters: Broad Asia risk-on supports FII sentiment toward Indian equities. Strong Chinese industrial momentum (PMI 51.8 in May) is positive for metals demand. Nikkei strength reflects global manufacturing recovery, a positive backdrop for India's capex and industrial cycle.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude -2.23% overnight on US-Iran 60-day peace roadmap
picks.logic: Jet fuel constitutes 35-40% of IndiGo's total operating costs. At ~$74/bbl WTI, fuel is well below IndiGo's budget assumptions. Every $1 crude drop saves IndiGo ~₹350-400 Cr/year in fuel. With Iran deal reducing geopolitical risk premium, crude may remain suppressed for weeks. IndiGo commands ~60% domestic market share and has strong operational leverage. Direct P&L benefit is immediate and significant.

picks.catalyst: Nasdaq +3.07% on June 22; USD/INR at 94.25 (structurally weak rupee)
picks.logic: Infosys derives ~85% of revenues from USD. Nasdaq's 3.07% surge — largest single-day gain in months — drives valuation re-rating for Indian IT. Infosys already gained 1.29% on June 22; momentum building. USD/INR at 94.25 vs historical ~83-84 means every USD dollar of revenue is worth ~13% more in INR. FII inflows (₹4,859 Cr) directly support high-FII-ownership stocks like INFY. Q1 FY27 results season beginning — strong deal pipeline expected.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude -2.23%; synthetic rubber and carbon black (crude derivatives) = 60-70% of RM cost
picks.logic: Apollo Tyres sources synthetic rubber and carbon black — both petroleum-derived — as 60-70% of raw material costs. Crude at $74 (vs ~$92 in Jan 2026) means RM basket is significantly cheaper. Every 5% crude fall improves Apollo's EBITDA margin by ~150-200bps. European operations add a USD/EUR revenue layer. At ₹425.50, stock is near lower end of its 52W range (₹365–₹540) offering asymmetric upside. Crude catalyst is more structural (Iran deal) not just a one-day blip.

risks: If Nifty gaps up >1% at open (GIFT Nifty suggesting ~0.74%), watch zones for all picks shift — reduce size by 50% on gap-opens
US-Iran deal is a 60-day roadmap, not a finalized agreement — any breakdown reverses crude gains sharply and punishes airlines/tyres
Fed's hawkish tilt (9/19 members favour rate hike in late 2026) — dollar strength risk could trigger FII reversal in coming sessions
Hang Seng death cross is a technical warning — if HSI breaks down, FII risk-off could spread to India metals and broader market
RBI inflation outlook upgraded to 5.1% — limits scope for rate cuts; NBFCs/real estate miss a catalyst
Not an expiry day (Thursday = weekly options expiry) — normal volatility expected; no elevated gamma risk today
